name: Walter Donaldson
abstract: Walter Donaldson (February 15, 1893 – July 15, 1947) was a prolific American popular songwriter and publishing company founder, composing many hit songs of the 1910s to 1940s, that have become standards and form part of the Great American Songbook.

works_created.t: Reaching for Someone (And Not Finding Anyone There)
Just Like a Melody Out of the Sky
My Baby Just Cares for Me
How Ya Gonna Keep 'em Down on the Farm (After They've Seen Paree?)

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: The repertoire is Jerome Kern, Johnny Mercer, Duke Ellington, Richard Rogers, Cole Porter, Walter Donaldson, people like that...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: That was our record, and then they gave me a tune by the name of 'Changes,' a Walter Donaldson tune, awfully nice.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Bailey and John McNeil
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...it was Walter Donaldson. [INTERVIEWEE] That fellow that I told you. He dropped there with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Bailey and John McNeil Interview, 1959-10-25
